AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Organization
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_COLUMN.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Organization
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_COLUMN.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Payment
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Payment event
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Payment event

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Created By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created By field indicates the user who created this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Payment
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Payment event
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Payment event

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Search Key
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A fast method for finding a particular record.
AD_COLUMN.HELP:
A search key allows for a fast method of finding a particular record. If you leave the search key empty, the system automatically creates a numeric number originating from a document sequence defined in the "Document Sequence" window. The sequence naming convention follows a rule "DocumentNo_TableName", where TableName is the actual name of the database table (e.g. C_Order).
Usually, company's internal identifiers for various records (products, customers, etc) are stored here.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Organization
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_COLUMN.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Lot
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A group of identical or similar items organized and placed into inventory under one number.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Lot Number indicates the specific lot that a product was part of.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Type
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A distinct item characteristic used for processes and sometimes grouped within a category.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Type indicates the type of validation that will occur. This can be SQL, Java Script or Java Language.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Creation Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Created By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created By field indicates the user who created this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Created By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created By field indicates the user who created this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Description
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A space to write additional related information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A description is limited to 255 characters.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Search Key
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A fast method for finding a particular record.
AD_COLUMN.HELP:
A search key allows for a fast method of finding a particular record. If you leave the search key empty, the system automatically creates a numeric number originating from a document sequence defined in the "Document Sequence" window. The sequence naming convention follows a rule "DocumentNo_TableName", where TableName is the actual name of the database table (e.g. C_Order).
Usually, company's internal identifiers for various records (products, customers, etc) are stored here.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Created By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created By field indicates the user who created this record.

AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Invoice
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A document listing products, quantities and prices, payment terms, etc.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Invoice ID uniquely identifies an Invoice Document.

AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Start Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date on which an item is due to start.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The date on which an item is due to start.

AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A group of identical or similar items organized and placed into inventory under one number.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Lot Number indicates the specific lot that a product was part of.

AD_COLUMN.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Summary Level
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A means of grouping fields in order to view or hide additional information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A summary entity represents a branch in a tree rather than an end-node. Summary entities are used for reporting and do not have own values.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Invoice
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A document listing products, quantities and prices, payment terms, etc.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Invoice ID uniquely identifies an Invoice Document.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Name
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A non-unique identifier for a record/document often used as a search tool.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A more descriptive identifier (that does need to be unique) of a record/document that is used as a default search option along with the search key (that is unique and mostly shorter). It is up to 60 characters in length.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Total
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Total amount

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Summary Level
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A means of grouping fields in order to view or hide additional information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A summary entity represents a branch in a tree rather than an end-node. Summary entities are used for reporting and do not have own values.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Grand Total Amount
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The final monetary amount (including taxes) charge listed in a document.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Grand Total displays the total amount including Tax and Freight in document currency

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Creation Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Document Type
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A value defining what sequence and process setup are used to handle this document.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Document Type determines document sequence and processing rules

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Organization
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_COLUMN.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Description
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A space to write additional related information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A description is limited to 255 characters.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Reference No.
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The number for a specific reference.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The reference number can be printed on orders and invoices to allow your business partner to faster identify your records.

AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who created this records

AD_COLUMN.HELP: Unique identifier and a reference to a Sales Order originating from the document sequence defined for this document type.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Search Key
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A fast method for finding a particular record.
AD_COLUMN.HELP:
A search key allows for a fast method of finding a particular record. If you leave the search key empty, the system automatically creates a numeric number originating from a document sequence defined in the "Document Sequence" window. The sequence naming convention follows a rule "DocumentNo_TableName", where TableName is the actual name of the database table (e.g. C_Order).
Usually, company's internal identifiers for various records (products, customers, etc) are stored here.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Creation Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Alert Status
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Identifies the Status of the document
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Identifies the Status of the document

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Summary Level
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A means of grouping fields in order to view or hide additional information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A summary entity represents a branch in a tree rather than an end-node. Summary entities are used for reporting and do not have own values.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Description
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A space to write additional related information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A description is limited to 255 characters.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Transaction Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date the transaction is carried out and registered in the cash journal.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Statement Date field defines the date of the statement being processed.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Lot Name
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A group of identical or similar items organized and placed into inventory under one number.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Lot Number indicates the specific lot that a product was part of.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Summed Line Amount
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The final sum of all line totals made to a specified document or transaction (not including taxes).
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Total amount displays the total of all lines in document currency.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Amount
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A monetary total.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Amount indicates the amount for this document line.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.HELP: Unique identifier and a reference to a Sales Order originating from the document sequence defined for this document type.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Description
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A space to write additional related information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A description is limited to 255 characters.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Summary Level
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A means of grouping fields in order to view or hide additional information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A summary entity represents a branch in a tree rather than an end-node. Summary entities are used for reporting and do not have own values.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Creation Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Summary Level
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A means of grouping fields in order to view or hide additional information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A summary entity represents a branch in a tree rather than an end-node. Summary entities are used for reporting and do not have own values.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Organization
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_COLUMN.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Type
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A distinct item characteristic used for processes and sometimes grouped within a category.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Type indicates the type of validation that will occur. This can be SQL, Java Script or Java Language.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Creation Date
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record is completed.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Created field indicates the date that this record was created.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Organization
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_COLUMN.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: G/L Item
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: An alias for the Account Combination which can be commonly used in daily operations.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: General ledger item.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Summary Level
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A means of grouping fields in order to view or hide additional information.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A summary entity represents a branch in a tree rather than an end-node. Summary entities are used for reporting and do not have own values.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Payment Terms
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The setup and timing defined to complete a specified payment.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Payment Terms identify the method and timing of payment for this transaction.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Organization
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_COLUMN.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Client
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Payment
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: Payment event
AD_COLUMN.HELP: Payment event

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Active
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Document No.
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: An often automatically generated identifier for all documents.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The document number is usually automatically generated by the system and determined by the document type of the document. If the document is not saved, the preliminary number is displayed in "<>". If the document type of your document has no automatic document sequence defined, the field will be empty when creating a new document. This is for documents which usually have an external number (like vendor invoice). If you leave the field empty, the system will generate a document number for you. The document sequence used for this fallback number is defined in the "Document Sequence" window with the name "DocumentNo_<TableName>", where TableName is the actual name of the table inside the database (e.g. C_Order).

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: The date that this record was last updated
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated field indicates the date that this record was updated.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Amount
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: A monetary total.
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Amount indicates the amount for this document line.

AD_COLUMN.NAME: Updated By
AD_COLUMN.DESCRIPTION: User who updated this records
AD_COLUMN.HELP: The Updated By field indicates the user who updated this record.
